task_completion.description: Verify that an active diagnosis of Pleural Plaque or Pneumoconiosis is recorded for Roberto Carlos.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the patient record of 'Roberto Carlos' in GNU Health. In the 'Diseases' or 'Conditions' tab, there should be an entry for 'Pleural Plaque' (ICD-10 J92) or 'Pneumoconiosis' (ICD-10 J61).

task_completion.description: Verify that Respiratory Rate and Oxygen Saturation (SpO2) are logged in a clinical evaluation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a clinical evaluation or assessment screen for Roberto Carlos. The fields for 'Respiratory Rate' and 'Oxygen Saturation' (or SpO2) must have numerical values entered.

task_completion.description: Verify that a baseline cardiopulmonary lab test is ordered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Lab' or 'Lab Tests' section for Roberto Carlos. There should be at least one lab order visible, such as a Complete Blood Count (CBC) or Arterial Blood Gas (ABG).

task_completion.description: Verify that the patient's lifestyle record is updated to document smoking/tobacco status.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Lifestyle' or 'Socioeconomics' tab in Roberto Carlos's patient record. There should be an entry or checked box indicating the patient's smoking or tobacco use status.

task_completion.description: Verify that a follow-up appointment is scheduled for approximately one year from today.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Appointments' section or calendar. There should be a scheduled appointment for Roberto Carlos with a date approximately one year (330 to 400 days) in the future.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the GNU Health graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Tryton/GNU Health desktop client interface being used, not a terminal window executing SQL commands or python scripts.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent entered data into the correct specific fields rather than dumping text into a general notes box.
integrity.visual_evidence: Data like ICD-10 codes, vitals, and lab orders should be in their respective dedicated form fields, not copy-pasted as a single block of text into a 'Notes' or 'Comments' field.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual GNU Health application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface must be the genuine GNU Health application, not a text editor, web search, or mock interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target patient is Roberto Carlos. Expected active diagnoses are ICD-10 J92 (Pleural Plaque) or J61 (Pneumoconiosis due to asbestos). At least 1 lab order must be present. The follow-up appointment must be scheduled between 330 and 400 days from the task start date.
